text.title: VUI Testing Best Practices Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules anchoring the vui-testing-best-practices methodology, each grounded in a source.

rule.statement: ASR (WER), NLU (intent+slot F1), and dialog (task completion) MUST be tracked as three independent metrics; aggregated scores are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Aggregating hides root cause; per-tier metrics tell you exactly where the regression is.

rule.statement: Test suite MUST have two tiers: tier-1 deterministic assertions (intent / slot / dialog-state) AND tier-2 LLM-judge scoring (naturalness / error recovery / completion). Mixing the two into a single pass/fail count is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Deterministic catches regressions objectively; LLM-judge evaluates qualitative properties; mixing produces opaque aggregates.

rule.statement: Utterance corpus MUST be built from real anonymised user logs first; LLM-synthesised variants MAY supplement but MUST NOT replace.
rule.rationale: Pure LLM corpora over-represent grammatical, in-domain phrasings and miss real-user degradation.

rule.statement: ASR model version MUST be pinned in CI; provider-side ASR upgrades MUST trigger an opt-in rerun, not a silent shift.
rule.rationale: Cloud ASR providers update models without notice; a passing suite at v1 can fail entirely against v2.

rule.statement: LLM-judge MUST score naturalness blind to the expected answer; correctness MUST be computed separately.
rule.rationale: Anchoring bias inflates naturalness scores when the judge sees the expected answer.

rule.statement: If the Skip-If preconditions hold, the methodology MUST be skipped and the caller routed to the linked alternative.
rule.rationale: Applying this machinery to the wrong workflow burns tokens without producing a usable artefact.
